create_spinning_doners.ponder.cooking_st: The Döner Station cooks a full döner when spun by horizontal or vertical shafts. It needs at least 64 RPM.
Raw Full Döners must be supplied through a Funnel on the input face, the block cannot be loaded by hand.
Cooking speed scales with RPM: 64, 128, 192, and 256+ each unlock a faster heat tier.
When finished, a Cooked Full Döner is ejected through the output Funnel.
Use a Wrench on the left or right side to toggle the horizontal shaft ports.
Use a non-extracting Funnel on the input face and an extracting Funnel on the output face.
create_spinning_doners.ponder.doner_spin: The Döner Spinner is a 2-block tall machine powered from below.
Right-click the upper half with a Raw Full Döner to load it.
Once loaded, it can be manually chopped using Shears, Swords, or any Knife.
Each Full Döner contains 50 pieces. Manual chopping works even when raw.
Right-click with an empty hand to collect output from the internal inventory.
Equip Engineer's Goggles to see detailed info like remaining chops and cooking progress.
Applying a Redstone signal enables Auto-Chopping.
By default, auto-chopping only occurs once the döner is cooked.
You can change this behavior by scrolling on the side of the machine.
'Always' mode allows auto-chopping raw pieces, while 'When Cooked' waits for heat.
Use a Wrench on the front or back to toggle the visual port status.
Automated input and output requires Funnels or Hoppers.
The front face (North) accepts Raw Full Döners, while the back (South) allows extraction.
create_spinning_doners.ponder.sideways_h: The Sideways Heater cooks döners from the side.
It must be placed facing the UPPER half of a Döner Spinner.
If the heater is removed, cooking progress will slowly revert to raw.
Wrench the back of the heater to enable a pass-through shaft.
A raw döner sits on the spinner. The heater detects the spinner's upper half and adds its cooking speed (based on RPM) to the cooking progress every tick
Higher RPM tiers dramatically shorten cook time, tier 12 (256+ RPM) cooks 12× faster than tier 1 (64 RPM). The cooking threshold is 1,200 ticks total
If the heater loses power or is removed, cooking progress decreases by 2 per tick, if it reaches 0 the döner reverts to raw. Keep it powered!
The döner is now fully cooked (COOKED state), pieces can be cut manually with a knife or automatically with a redstone signal on the spinner
Remember: the heater draws 64 SU constantly, plan your stress network to handle this load. Consider a creative motor or a dedicated steam engine for testing
